summary: The test suite provides solid coverage of major functionality (patch/minor/major bumps, both file formats, changelog generation, error handling) with 17 unit tests and 17 workflow structure tests. However, it has significant gaps: (1) **Requirement violation**: Requirements explicitly state 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT' and 'Do NOT test your script directly,' but test-version-bumper.bats invokes the script directly without workflow mediation; (2) **Coverage gaps**: Scoped commits (feat(scope):) work in implementation but aren't explicitly tested; BREAKING CHANGE footer format mentioned in comments but not distinctly validated; empty commit files, malformed semver, and whitespace edge cases unaddressed; (3) **Rigor issues**: The act integration test checks only version number outputs (PATCH_RESULT, MINOR_RESULT, etc.) but does NOT validate changelog content; per-test-case act invocations not implemented (requirement called for separate act run per test case); (4) **Design issues**: The act test bundles 6 assertions into one monolithic test; fixture content not verified; no helper functions to reduce duplication. The test suite would catch regressions in happy-path scenarios but miss subtle bugs in boundary conditions, special characters, or workflow environment issues.
